question: [File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / IMidnight.setIsAuthorized] Can an unprivileged attacker who holds a valid EcrecoverAuthorizer signature for authorization.isAuthorized=true, under the precondition that the authorizer has pre-authorized the EcrecoverAuthorizer contract in Midnight, submit the signature to grant themselves full Midnight control over the authorizer's position, then call updatePosition on the authorizer's lender position to force loss-factor slashing at an attacker-chosen time, violating the invariant that credit slashing must only occur at the position owner's next voluntary interaction, causing scoped impact: unfair credit destruction for the authorizer-lender? Proof idea: fuzz lossFactor delta and timing of updatePosition call; assert credit(authorizer) after forced updatePosition equals credit(authorizer) after voluntary interaction.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / IMidnight.liquidate] Can an unprivileged attacker who obtains authorization over a victim lender via EcrecoverAuthorizer, under the precondition that the market has accumulated bad debt and the victim has non-zero credit, call liquidate(seizedAssets=0, repaidUnits=0) on a zero-collateral borrower to realize bad debt and update lossFactor, then immediately call updatePosition on the victim's position to apply the freshly realized loss, violating the invariant that bad debt must be socialized proportionally and not front-run by a single authorized actor, causing scoped impact: bad debt misallocation where the attacker times the slashing to maximize the victim's credit loss? Proof idea: compare victim credit loss when updatePosition is called immediately after bad debt vs. after other lenders have also been updated.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / isAuthorized delegation chain] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) exploit the check signer == authorization.authorizer || IMidnight(MIDNIGHT).isAuthorized(authorization.authorizer, signer) to sign a new Authorization struct on behalf of the original authorizer, granting a third address authorization, under the precondition that the original authorizer authorized the operator in Midnight, creating an irrevocable delegation chain that persists even after the original authorization is revoked, violating the invariant that authorization revocation must fully remove the authorized party's ability to act, causing scoped impact: position desynchronization where an unauthorized third party can trigger loss-factor accounting on the victim's position? Proof idea: unit test showing that revoking operator A does not prevent A's previously-granted sub-authorization to B from persisting.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized nonce management] Can an attacker front-run a victim's revocation signature (nonce N+1, isAuthorized=false) by submitting the victim's previously signed grant signature (nonce N, isAuthorized=true) first, under the precondition that both signatures are in the mempool simultaneously, consuming nonce N and invalidating the revocation attempt, violating the invariant that a user's intent to revoke authorization must be enforceable, causing scoped impact: an unauthorized operator retains full Midnight control and can trigger loss-factor accounting on the victim's position indefinitely? Proof idea: simulate mempool ordering; assert that after front-run, nonce[authorizer] == N+1 and isAuthorized[authorizer][attacker] == true.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / Midnight.take] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) call take on behalf of a lender-buyer, under the precondition that the buyer has no credit (hasCredit returns false) and units <= buyerPos.debt, bypassing the _updatePosition call for the buyer (since the condition hasCredit(id, buyer) || units > buyerPos.debt is false), leaving lastLossFactor stale, then after bad debt is realized in the market, call updatePosition to apply the full accumulated loss-factor delta, violating the invariant that _updatePosition must be called before any credit modification, causing scoped impact: incorrect loss-factor application leading to more credit destruction than the position's fair share? Proof idea: fuzz units and buyerPos.debt near the boundary; assert lastLossFactor is updated correctly when take bypasses _updatePosition.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / Midnight.withdraw] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) call withdraw(units=0) on a lender's position, under the precondition that the market has a non-zero lossFactor delta since the lender's lastLossFactor, forcing _updatePosition to execute and slash the lender's credit without any actual withdrawal, violating the invariant that credit slashing should not be triggerable by a zero-value operation initiated by a third party, causing scoped impact: unfair credit destruction where the attacker chooses the exact moment of slashing to maximize loss? Proof idea: assert that withdraw(0) triggers _updatePosition and reduces credit by the loss-factor delta amount.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / Midnight.multicall] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) use Midnight.multicall to atomically: (1) call liquidate(seizedAssets=0, repaidUnits=0) to realize bad debt and increase lossFactor, (2) call updatePosition on a target lender to apply the new loss, (3) call withdraw on the target lender's position to extract the remaining credit, under the precondition that the operator is authorized for both the liquidator role and the lender's position, violating the invariant that bad debt socialization must not be exploitable for targeted credit extraction, causing scoped impact: credit destruction and unauthorized withdrawal from a victim lender's position? Proof idea: invariant test asserting that no single multicall can both realize bad debt and withdraw from a specific lender's position.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / _updatePosition lossFactor delta] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) manipulate the sequence of updatePosition calls across multiple lenders in the same market, under the precondition that multiple bad debt events have occurred between lender interactions, causing some lenders to have their lastLossFactor updated at intermediate values while others absorb the full cumulative loss, violating the invariant that bad debt must reduce lender credit proportionally and uniformly, causing scoped impact: bad debt misallocation where some lenders absorb disproportionately more loss than others? Proof idea: fuzz the order of updatePosition calls across N lenders; assert sum(credit_i_after) is consistent regardless of call order.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / lossFactor near type(uint128).max] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) trigger repeated bad debt realizations via liquidate(0,0) calls on multiple zero-collateral borrowers, under the precondition that totalUnits is small and each bad debt event pushes lossFactor closer to type(uint128).max, causing the denominator (type(uint128).max - lastLossFactor) in _updatePosition to approach zero and produce extreme rounding errors, violating the invariant that credit slashing must be proportional to actual bad debt, causing scoped impact: credit destruction exceeding actual bad debt due to fixed-point precision loss near the lossFactor ceiling? Proof idea: fuzz totalUnits in range [1, 1000] and badDebt near totalUnits; assert sum of slashed credit equals badDebt within acceptable rounding.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / continuousFeeCredit scaling] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) trigger bad debt realization at a time when continuousFeeCredit is large, under the precondition that the market has accumulated significant continuous fees and lossFactor is non-zero, causing continuousFeeCredit to be scaled down by the formula continuousFeeCredit.mulDivDown(MAX - newLossFactor, MAX - oldLossFactor), violating the invariant that fee credit must not be destroyed by bad debt events beyond the proportional share, causing scoped impact: disproportionate continuousFeeCredit destruction leading to unclaimable protocol fees? Proof idea: assert continuousFeeCredit after bad debt realization equals continuousFeeCredit_before * (1 - badDebt/totalUnits) within rounding.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / Midnight.repay + updatePosition] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) call repay on behalf of a borrower to reduce their debt to zero, then call updatePosition on a lender's position to apply accumulated lossFactor, under the precondition that the borrower had bad debt that was already socialized into lossFactor before repayment, causing the lender's credit to be slashed for bad debt that was subsequently repaid, violating the invariant that repaid debt must not contribute to lender credit loss, causing scoped impact: unfair debt reduction where lenders lose credit for debt that was actually repaid? Proof idea: assert that repay followed by updatePosition does not slash lender credit for the repaid amount.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / EIP-712 domain separator] Can an attacker replay an EcrecoverAuthorizer signature on a second EcrecoverAuthorizer deployment at a different address on the same chain, under the precondition that the victim has authorized both EcrecoverAuthorizer instances in Midnight, exploiting the fact that the domain separator includes address(this) but not the Midnight contract address, allowing the same signature to grant authorization via either instance, violating the invariant that each EcrecoverAuthorizer deployment must have an independent signature namespace, causing scoped impact: unauthorized authorization grant enabling loss-factor manipulation on the victim's position? Proof idea: deploy two EcrecoverAuthorizer instances; assert that a signature valid for instance A is rejected by instance B.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / ecrecover address(0) check] Can an attacker craft a malformed signature (e.g., s=0, r=0, v=27) that causes ecrecover to return address(0), under the precondition that authorization.authorizer is set to address(0) (a zero-address authorizer), bypassing the require(signer != address(0)) check since signer == authorization.authorizer == address(0), and granting authorization to an arbitrary address in Midnight's isAuthorized mapping for the zero-address key, violating the invariant that invalid signatures must always revert, causing scoped impact: position desynchronization if any protocol logic uses address(0) as a default or fallback authorizer? Proof idea: unit test with authorization.authorizer=address(0) and a zero-value signature; assert revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / Midnight.take seller _updatePosition skip] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) call take on behalf of a seller who has no credit (hasCredit returns false), under the precondition that the seller has accumulated debt and the market has a non-zero lossFactor delta since the seller's lastLossFactor, bypassing the _updatePosition call for the seller (since hasCredit(id, seller) is false), leaving the seller's lastLossFactor stale, then after the take increases the seller's debt, call updatePosition to apply the full historical loss-factor delta to a now-larger credit base, violating the invariant that loss-factor must be applied before any position modification, causing scoped impact: incorrect loss-factor application leading to position desynchronization? Proof idea: assert that after take with no-credit seller, subsequent updatePosition applies correct loss-factor delta.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / Midnight.liquidate badDebt calculation] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) call liquidate with collateralIndex pointing to a collateral not in the borrower's collateralBitmap, under the precondition that seizedAssets=0 and repaidUnits=0, causing the badDebt loop to iterate only over active collaterals while the liquidated collateral price is 0 (liquidatedCollatPrice never set), and the bad debt calculation to proceed with an incorrect collateral valuation, violating the invariant that bad debt must equal originalDebt minus the maximum recoverable value across all collaterals, causing scoped impact: bad debt misallocation where lossFactor is updated with an incorrect badDebt value? Proof idea: fuzz collateralIndex against the borrower's collateralBitmap; assert badDebt calculation is consistent.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / Midnight.setIsAuthorized authorization check] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) call Midnight.setIsAuthorized to grant authorization to a new address B on behalf of the original authorizer, then have B call EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized using a signature where signer == B and IMidnight.isAuthorized(authorizer, B) == true, under the precondition that the original authorizer authorized the EcrecoverAuthorizer contract in Midnight, creating a recursive authorization expansion where each authorized party can grant further authorizations, violating the invariant that the original authorizer's intent must bound the set of authorized parties, causing scoped impact: position desynchronization where an unbounded set of addresses can trigger loss-factor accounting on the victim? Proof idea: trace authorization chain depth; assert that revocation of the root authorization prevents all downstream authorized parties from acting.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / _updatePosition lastLossFactor sync] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) call updatePosition on a lender's position immediately after a bad debt event, under the precondition that the lender's lastLossFactor equals the pre-bad-debt lossFactor, causing the credit to be slashed by the full bad debt delta, then have the lender call updatePosition again (no-op since lastLossFactor is now current), and compare this to a scenario where the lender calls updatePosition before the bad debt event, violating the invariant that the timing of updatePosition must not affect the total credit loss, causing scoped impact: unfair credit destruction where forced early updatePosition causes more loss than voluntary late updatePosition? Proof idea: differential test comparing credit(lender) after forced vs. voluntary updatePosition timing.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / Midnight.liquidate totalUnits underflow] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) trigger multiple sequential bad debt realizations via liquidate(0,0) calls, under the precondition that each call reduces totalUnits by badDebt, causing totalUnits to approach zero while lossFactor approaches type(uint128).max, and the final bad debt realization to set totalUnits=0 while lossFactor=type(uint128).max, violating the invariant that take must revert when lossFactor is maxed out (MarketLossFactorMaxedOut), causing scoped impact: market freeze where no further takes can occur, permanently locking all remaining lender credit? Proof idea: fuzz sequence of liquidate(0,0) calls; assert market becomes frozen and remaining credit is unwithdrawable.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / Midnight.withdraw credit underflow] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) call withdraw on a lender's position after forcing _updatePosition via a zero-value operation, under the precondition that the loss-factor slashing reduces the lender's credit below the requested withdrawal amount, causing the credit subtraction to underflow (credit -= units where units > post-slash credit), violating the invariant that withdraw must not allow credit to go negative, causing scoped impact: transaction revert that freezes the lender's ability to withdraw their remaining credit? Proof idea: assert that withdraw(units) reverts when units > credit after loss-factor application.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / Midnight.take _updatePosition conditional] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) craft a take where the buyer has exactly units == buyerPos.debt (so buyerCreditIncrease == 0 and the condition units > buyerPos.debt is false), under the precondition that hasCredit(id, buyer) is false, bypassing _updatePosition for the buyer, then immediately after the take call updatePosition to apply accumulated lossFactor, violating the invariant that _updatePosition must be called before any position modification that could be affected by lossFactor, causing scoped impact: position desynchronization where the buyer's lastLossFactor is not updated before their debt is reduced? Proof idea: fuzz units == buyerPos.debt boundary; assert lastLossFactor is correctly synced after take.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / Midnight.liquidate post-maturity mode] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) call liquidate in post-maturity mode on a healthy borrower's position, under the precondition that block.timestamp > market.maturity and the borrower has non-zero debt, triggering bad debt realization even though the borrower is healthy (has sufficient collateral), causing lossFactor to increase and slashing all lenders' credit, violating the invariant that bad debt must only be realized when collateral is genuinely insufficient to cover debt, causing scoped impact: bad debt misallocation where healthy borrowers' debt is incorrectly socialized to lenders? Proof idea: assert that post-maturity liquidation of a healthy position does not realize bad debt (badDebt should be 0 when collateral covers debt at maxLif).

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / Midnight.liquidate badDebt loop maxLif] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) trigger liquidation on a multi-collateral borrower where one collateral's oracle returns a price that causes mulDivUp overflow in the badDebt calculation (_collateral.mulDivUp(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivUp(WAD, _collateralParam.maxLif)), under the precondition that the collateral amount and price are near type(uint128).max, causing the bad debt loop to revert or produce an incorrect badDebt value, violating the invariant that badDebt must equal originalDebt minus the sum of collateral values at maxLif, causing scoped impact: bad debt misallocation or liquidation DoS? Proof idea: fuzz collateral amounts and oracle prices near overflow boundaries; assert badDebt calculation is correct.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / Midnight.liquidate lossFactor formula rounding] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) trigger bad debt realization with badDebt == 1 (minimum), under the precondition that totalUnits is very large (near type(uint128).max), causing the lossFactor update formula type(uint128).max - (type(uint128).max - _lossFactor).mulDivDown(_totalUnits - 1, _totalUnits) to round up by at most 1 unit, and repeating this N times to accumulate rounding errors that cause lenders to collectively lose more credit than the actual bad debt, violating the invariant that total credit slashed must equal total bad debt within acceptable rounding, causing scoped impact: unfair credit destruction exceeding actual bad debt? Proof idea: fuzz N sequential bad debt events with badDebt=1; assert sum(credit_slashed) <= sum(badDebt) + N.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / Midnight.liquidate seizedAssets=0 repaidUnits=0] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) call liquidate(seizedAssets=0, repaidUnits=0) on a borrower with non-zero debt and non-zero collateral, under the precondition that the borrower is unhealthy, causing bad debt to be realized (lossFactor updated) without any collateral being seized or debt being repaid, violating the invariant that bad debt realization must only occur after all collateral has been exhausted, causing scoped impact: bad debt misallocation where lossFactor is increased while the borrower still has collateral that could cover the debt? Proof idea: assert that liquidate(0,0) with non-zero collateral does not increase lossFactor.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect loss-factor accounting causing unfair debt reduction, credit destruction, bad debt misallocation, or position desynchronization'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / Midnight.take lossFactor maxed check] Can an authorized operator (granted via EcrecoverAuthorizer) push lossFactor to type(uint128).max via repeated bad debt realizations, then attempt to call take on behalf of the authorizer, under the precondition that the market's lossFactor == type(uint128).max, causing take to revert with MarketLossFactorMaxedOut, permanently freezing the authorizer's ability to trade in the market, violating the invariant that a market must remain accessible for unwinding even after bad debt, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where the authorizer cannot exit their position? Proof idea: assert that after lossFactor reaches max, all take calls revert and credit cannot be withdrawn.
